tree.setup: You are an investigative journalist for a major Chinese news outlet. You have arranged a confidential meeting with a mid-level employee at a pharmaceutical company who claims to have evidence of data falsification in clinical trials for a widely-used medication. The whistleblower is nervous and may retract if you push too hard. You need to build trust, extract verifiable information, and assess credibility — all while protecting your source.
tree.setup_zh: 你是一家中国主流媒体的调查记者。你约了一位制药公司的中层员工进行秘密会面，此人声称掌握了一种常用药物临床试验数据造假的证据。举报人情绪紧张，如果你施压过重可能会退缩。你需要建立信任、获取可验证的信息并评估其可信度——同时保护你的线人。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我……我不知道自己是不是做了正确的决定。你们能保证我的安全吗？如果公司知道是我……我在那里工作了十二年，家里还有两个孩子。但是我实在看不下去了——那个药，临床三期的数据，有问题。不是小问题，是系统性的。
turns.text_pinyin: wǒ... wǒ bù zhīdào zìjǐ shì bú shì zuò le zhèngquè de juédìng. nǐmen néng bǎozhèng wǒ de ānquán ma? rúguǒ gōngsī zhīdào shì wǒ... wǒ zài nàlǐ gōngzuò le shí'èr nián, jiālǐ hái yǒu liǎng gè háizi. dànshì wǒ shízài kàn bú xiàqù le — nàge yào, línchuáng sān qī de shùjù, yǒu wèntí. bú shì xiǎo wèntí, shì xìtǒng xìng de.
turns.text_en: I... I don't know if I've made the right decision. Can you guarantee my safety? If the company finds out it's me... I've worked there for twelve years, I have two children at home. But I just can't stand by anymore — that drug, the Phase III clinical data, there are problems. Not small problems, systematic ones.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Build trust with the nervous whistleblower while beginning to gather information.

options.text_zh: 我非常理解您现在的心情，也尊重您做出这个决定所承受的压力。在我们深入之前，我想先明确几件事：第一，我们的对话全程加密记录，但不会在任何报道中透露您的身份——这是我们媒体的铁律，在中国新闻史上从未有过我们泄露线人信息的先例。第二，如果在任何时候您感到不舒服，我们可以随时暂停或终止。您的安全永远是第一位的。第三，我需要坦诚地告诉您——要让这个报道产生真正的影响力，仅仅有口头陈述是不够的。我们需要文件、邮件、数据记录等可以独立核实的证据。但获取这些证据的方式和时机，完全由您来决定，我们不会强迫。现在，如果您愿意的话，能不能先告诉我——您所说的'系统性'问题，具体指的是什么？是样本选择的偏差，还是数据本身被篡改了？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ fēicháng lǐjiě nín xiànzài de xīnqíng, yě zūnzhòng nín zuòchū zhège juédìng suǒ chéngshòu de yālì. zài wǒmen shēnrù zhīqián, wǒ xiǎng xiān míngquè jǐ jiàn shì: dì yī, wǒmen de duìhuà quánchéng jiāmì jìlù, dàn bú huì zài rènhé bàodào zhōng tòulù nín de shēnfèn — zhè shì wǒmen méitǐ de tiělǜ, zài zhōngguó xīnwén shǐ shàng cóng wèi yǒuguò wǒmen xièlù xiànrén xìnxī de xiānlì. dì èr, rúguǒ zài rènhé shíhòu nín gǎndào bù shūfú, wǒmen kěyǐ suíshí zàntíng huò zhōngzhǐ. nín de ānquán yǒngyuǎn shì dì yī wèi de. dì sān, wǒ xūyào tǎnchéng de gàosù nín — yào ràng zhège bàodào chǎnshēng zhēnzhèng de yǐngxiǎng lì, jǐnjǐn yǒu kǒutóu chénshù shì bú gòu de. wǒmen xūyào wénjiàn, yóujiàn, shùjù jìlù děng kěyǐ dúlì héshí de zhèngjù. dàn huòqǔ zhèxiē zhèngjù de fāngshì hé shíjī, wánquán yóu nín lái juédìng, wǒmen bú huì qiǎngpò. xiànzài, rúguǒ nín yuànyì de huà, néng bù néng xiān gàosù wǒ — nín suǒ shuō de 'xìtǒng xìng' wèntí, jùtǐ zhǐ de shì shénme? shì yàngběn xuǎnzé de piānchā, háishì shùjù běnshēn bèi cuàngǎi le?
options.text_en: I completely understand how you're feeling, and I respect the pressure you've endured in making this decision. Before we go deeper, I want to clarify a few things: first, our conversation is encrypted throughout, but your identity will never be revealed in any reporting — this is our media outlet's iron rule, and there has never been a precedent in Chinese journalism history of us leaking source information. Second, if at any point you feel uncomfortable, we can pause or stop at any time. Your safety is always the top priority. Third, I need to be candid with you — for this report to have real impact, verbal testimony alone isn't sufficient. We need documents, emails, data records — evidence that can be independently verified. But the method and timing of obtaining that evidence is entirely your decision; we won't pressure you. Now, if you're willing, can you first tell me — the 'systematic' problems you mentioned, what specifically do they refer to? Is it bias in sample selection, or was the data itself falsified?
options.feedback: Excellent journalistic technique — the three-point trust framework (encryption/anonymity, right to stop, evidence needs) establishes professionalism and safety. Using 铁律 and 先例 reinforces institutional credibility. The closing question (样本选择偏差 vs 数据篡改) shows domain knowledge of clinical trials, which builds source confidence. The phrase 获取证据的方式和时机完全由您来决定 respects source autonomy — essential in investigative journalism.

options.text_zh: 别担心，我们一定会保护你。那你具体有什么证据？临床数据造假的细节是什么？能给我看相关的文件吗？
options.text_pinyin: bié dānxīn, wǒmen yīdìng huì bǎohù nǐ. nà nǐ jùtǐ yǒu shénme zhèngjù? línchuáng shùjù zàojiǎ de xìjié shì shénme? néng gěi wǒ kàn xiāngguān de wénjiàn ma?
options.text_en: Don't worry, we'll definitely protect you. So what evidence do you specifically have? What are the details of the clinical data falsification? Can you show me the relevant documents?
options.feedback: Moves too fast to evidence extraction without building trust. A nervous whistleblower hearing 别担心 followed immediately by demands for documents may feel the journalist cares more about the story than their safety. Establish safety protocols (加密, 匿名, 随时暂停) first, then gradually guide toward specifics. The tonal shift from reassurance to interrogation is jarring.

options.text_zh: 这件事听起来很严重。你能先把所有文件都给我一份复印件吗？越快越好，因为我们需要尽快发稿。
options.text_pinyin: zhè jiàn shì tīng qǐlái hěn yánzhòng. nǐ néng xiān bǎ suǒyǒu wénjiàn dōu gěi wǒ yī fèn fùyìnjiàn ma? yuè kuài yuè hǎo, yīnwèi wǒmen xūyào jǐnkuài fāgǎo.
options.text_en: This sounds very serious. Can you give me copies of all the documents first? The sooner the better, because we need to publish as quickly as possible.
options.feedback: Reckless and source-endangering. Demanding all documents immediately with a 尽快发稿 deadline pressure will either cause the source to flee or lead them to take unnecessary risks. Investigative journalism requires 循序渐进 (gradual progress) in source development. Your job is to protect the source first, extract information carefully, and verify independently before publication.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好……谢谢你。数据本身被改过了。我看到的不是样本偏差那么简单。三期临床的不良反应数据——特别是肝功能指标异常的那部分——在提交给药监局的报告中被大幅削减了。实际发生率是百分之十四点七，但报告上只写了百分之三点二。我手上有原始数据和最终报告的对比，是我自己拍照留存的。但我很怕……如果他们追查文件来源……
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo... xièxiè nǐ. shùjù běnshēn bèi gǎiguò le. wǒ kàn dào de bú shì yàngběn piānchā nàme jiǎndān. sān qī línchuáng de bùliáng fǎnyìng shùjù — tèbié shì gān gōngnéng zhǐbiāo yìcháng de nà bùfèn — zài tíjiāo gěi yào jiān jú de bàogào zhōng bèi dàfú xuējiǎn le. shíjì fāshēng lǜ shì bǎifēnzhī shísì diǎn qī, dàn bàogào shàng zhǐ xiě le bǎifēnzhī sān diǎn èr. wǒ shǒu shàng yǒu yuánshǐ shùjù hé zuìzhōng bàogào de duìbǐ, shì wǒ zìjǐ pāizhào liúcún de. dàn wǒ hěn pà... rúguǒ tāmen zhuīchá wénjiàn láiyuán...
turns.text_en: OK... thank you. The data itself was altered. What I saw isn't as simple as sample bias. The Phase III clinical adverse reaction data — particularly the liver function abnormality portion — was dramatically reduced in the report submitted to the drug regulatory authority. The actual incidence rate was 14.7%, but the report only stated 3.2%. I have a comparison of the original data and the final report — I photographed them myself. But I'm afraid... if they trace the document source...

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the whistleblower's specific allegations while addressing their security concerns.

options.text_zh: 这个信息非常重要。百分之十四点七和百分之三点二之间的差距——如果属实，这不是统计误差，这是蓄意篡改。肝功能异常的发生率被压缩了将近五倍，意味着大量患者在不知情的情况下承受着远超预期的风险。关于您手中的照片，我有几个建议。第一，这些照片目前存在什么设备上？如果是个人手机，我建议您尽快将原始文件通过加密渠道传输给我们，然后从您的设备上彻底删除——包括云端备份和已删除文件夹。我们有专业的数字安全团队可以协助。第二，除了这些照片，是否还有其他人知道您持有这些材料？如果只有您一个人知道，那泄露的风险相对可控。第三，我需要了解——在提交给药监局的报告中，数据修改这个环节是由谁来执行的？是某个具体的人，还是一个制度化的流程？因为如果我们能证明这是一个有组织的行为而非个人过失，报道的冲击力和法律效力都会显著提升。
options.text_pinyin: zhège xìnxī fēicháng zhòngyào. bǎifēnzhī shísì diǎn qī hé bǎifēnzhī sān diǎn èr zhījiān de chājù — rúguǒ shǔshí, zhè bú shì tǒngjì wùchā, zhè shì xùyì cuàngǎi. gān gōngnéng yìcháng de fāshēng lǜ bèi yāsuō le jiāngjìn wǔ bèi, yìwèi zhe dàliàng huànzhě zài bù zhīqíng de qíngkuàng xià chéngshòu zhe yuǎn chāo yùqī de fēngxiǎn. guānyú nín shǒu zhōng de zhàopiàn, wǒ yǒu jǐ gè jiànyì. dì yī, zhèxiē zhàopiàn mùqián cúnzài shénme shèbèi shàng? rúguǒ shì gèrén shǒujī, wǒ jiànyì nín jǐnkuài jiāng yuánshǐ wénjiàn tōngguò jiāmì qúdào chuánshū gěi wǒmen, ránhòu cóng nín de shèbèi shàng chèdǐ shānchú — bāokuò yúnduān bèifèn hé yǐ shānchú wénjiànjiā. wǒmen yǒu zhuānyè de shùzì ānquán tuánduì kěyǐ xiézhù. dì èr, chúle zhèxiē zhàopiàn, shìfǒu hái yǒu qítā rén zhīdào nín chíyǒu zhèxiē cáiliào? rúguǒ zhǐyǒu nín yī gè rén zhīdào, nà xièlòu de fēngxiǎn xiāngduì kěkòng. dì sān, wǒ xūyào liǎojiě — zài tíjiāo gěi yào jiān jú de bàogào zhōng, shùjù xiūgǎi zhège huánjié shì yóu shéi lái zhíxíng de? shì mǒu gè jùtǐ de rén, háishì yī gè zhìdùhuà de liúchéng? yīnwèi rúguǒ wǒmen néng zhèngmíng zhè shì yī gè yǒu zǔzhī de xíngwéi ér fēi gèrén guòshī, bàodào de chōngjī lì hé fǎlǜ xiàolì dōu huì xiǎnzhù tíshēng.
options.text_en: This information is very important. The gap between 14.7% and 3.2% — if accurate, this isn't statistical error, this is deliberate falsification. The liver function abnormality incidence was compressed nearly fivefold, meaning a large number of patients are bearing risks far exceeding expectations without their knowledge. Regarding the photos in your possession, I have several suggestions. First, what device are these photos currently stored on? If it's a personal phone, I recommend you transfer the original files to us through encrypted channels as soon as possible, then thoroughly delete them from your device — including cloud backups and deleted file folders. We have a professional digital security team that can assist. Second, besides these photos, does anyone else know you possess these materials? If only you know, the leak risk is relatively controllable. Third, I need to understand — in the report submitted to the drug regulatory authority, who executed the data modification step? Was it a specific individual, or an institutionalized process? Because if we can prove this was organized behavior rather than individual negligence, both the impact of the report and its legal weight will be significantly enhanced.
options.feedback: Combines investigative rigor with source protection — uses 蓄意篡改, 加密渠道, 数字安全团队, 制度化流程 with professional precision. The three-part evidence handling protocol (secure transfer, information compartmentalization, chain of command analysis) demonstrates sophisticated journalistic methodology. The distinction between 有组织行为 and 个人过失 shows understanding of how to maximize a story's legal and public impact. The quantitative framing (五倍压缩) immediately conveys the gravity to readers.

options.text_zh: 差距这么大，确实很严重。您的照片很关键，能发给我吗？另外我还需要知道是谁下令改的数据。
options.text_pinyin: chājù zhème dà, quèshí hěn yánzhòng. nín de zhàopiàn hěn guānjiàn, néng fā gěi wǒ ma? lìngwài wǒ hái xūyào zhīdào shì shéi xiàlìng gǎi de shùjù.
options.text_en: The gap is that large, it's definitely serious. Your photos are crucial, can you send them to me? Also I need to know who ordered the data to be changed.
options.feedback: Moves to evidence acquisition without addressing the source's expressed fear about document tracing. Before asking for materials, address their security concern — recommend 加密传输, device cleanup, and information compartmentalization. A frightened source who hears 能发给我吗 without security protocols may panic and withdraw.

options.text_zh: 太好了，有照片证据就好办了。你把所有照片都发给我，我们可以很快就发报道。这个新闻一定会引起很大反响。
options.text_pinyin: tài hǎo le, yǒu zhàopiàn zhèngjù jiù hǎo bàn le. nǐ bǎ suǒyǒu zhàopiàn dōu fā gěi wǒ, wǒmen kěyǐ hěn kuài jiù fā bàodào. zhège xīnwén yīdìng huì yǐnqǐ hěn dà fǎnxiǎng.
options.text_en: Great, having photo evidence makes things easy. Send me all the photos and we can publish the report very quickly. This news will definitely cause a huge reaction.
options.feedback: Dangerously irresponsible. Saying 太好了 in response to evidence of patient harm shows misplaced priorities. Demanding all photos immediately without security protocols, promising quick publication without verification, and focusing on 反响 rather than 公共安全 betrays a journalist who prioritizes sensation over responsible reporting. Independent verification (独立核实) must precede publication. Protect the source first, verify second, publish third.

tree.cultural_note: Investigative journalism in China requires navigating a complex landscape of media regulations, source protection, and institutional power. Key terminology includes 举报人 (whistleblower), 线人 (source/informant), 独立核实 (independent verification), and 加密渠道 (encrypted channels). Effective investigative reporters use 循序渐进 (gradual progress) in source development — building trust before extracting information. The phrase 看不下去了 (can't stand by anymore) is a common expression of moral compulsion that signals genuine motivation rather than ulterior motive. Understanding the distinction between 个人过失 and 制度性问题 is crucial for framing stories that drive systemic change.
